en: Some of you may or may not know I have a new job. This is great. Thank you very much. Thank you.
ko: 모르시는 분도 있겠지만 새 직장을 구했어요. 좋다. 감사합니다. 감사합니다.

en: So that's fun. I phoned my grandmother to tell her the good news, which was really cool.
ko: 그래서 재밌었어요. 할머니한테 전화해서 좋은 소식을 전했는데, 정말 좋았어요.

en: I don't think she understood what was going on, but still she was happy.
ko: 무슨 일인지 잘 이해 못 하신 것 같지만 그래도 기뻐하셨어요.

en: And she was on the phone. I was like, Granny, I'm going to be on The Daily Show.
ko: 전화로 이러셨어요. 할머니, The Daily Show에 나가게 됐어요.

en: And she's like, ooh, Trevor, I'm so happy for you. You got a job.
ko: 그러니까 할머니가, 오, Trevor, 정말 기쁘구나. 직장을 구했구나.

en: I said, no, no, no, no, Gogo. No, I already had a job, Gogo. I already had a job.
ko: 아니, 아니, 아니, 할머니. 아니요, 이미 직장이 있었어요, 할머니. 이미 직장이 있었다고요.

en: She's like, no, you didn't. Did you have an office?
I said, no. She's like, then it wasn't a job. Well done, Trevor.
ko: 아니요. 그럼 직장이 아니었어. 잘했다, Trevor.

en: My mom was a bit better. I phoned her. She was on the ball. You know, she was really excited.
ko: 엄마는 좀 나았어요. 전화했더니 상황 파악을 잘 하시더라고요. 정말 신나하셨어요.

en: And to give you a bit of a backstory, I have two younger brothers.
ko: 배경을 좀 알려드리면, 남동생이 두 명 있어요.

en: I have one brother who is nine years younger than me. And then the youngest is 20 years younger than me.
